artPrompt: The suspended instant of indecision: a silver coin balanced impossibly on its razor edge atop a goblin's outstretched claw, hovering between fates, faint flame-orange light shimmering around it. A single bonfire glow from below catches the coin's edge and the gambler's held breath, the feral town square behind dissolving into smoke-black shadow. Rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: Half a Coin Flip
text:
Flip a coin, but stop it on its edge before it lands. While it balances:
• You win half the flip. Deal 1½ damage to any target.
• You also lose half the flip. The same target deals 1½ damage back to you, simultaneously, as both players.
When the coin finally falls (it won't), resolve the other half. Leave the value as an exercise for the reader.
flavorText: Schrödinger's goblin: simultaneously winning and losing the election.

extras.impossibleMath: half a coin flip, 1½ damage, as both players simultaneously, value left to the reader
